items.title: 100m litres of diesel secured under new powers, PM confirms

children.content: Anthony Albanese says that new powers allowing the federal government to underwrite the delivery of additional fuel cargoes have been used to purchase 100 million litres of diesel.

articles.title: Why Australia turned to tiny nation to secure critical fertiliser supplies

children.content: The Middle East conflict has disrupted the flow of Australia's imports of the fertiliser urea. Facing the potential threat of reduced crop yields, Anthony Albanese looked to Australia's neighbours.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese in a grey and green jacket and white hard hat, smiling while holding up two jars of blue and brown urea.

gallery.altText: Two men sit at desk in front of a row of flags.

thumbnail.altText: Two men sit at desk in front of a row of flags.

items.title: Trump rules out extending ceasefire as Australia secures additional diesel

children.content: US President Donald Trump says a long-term peace deal with Iran is "preferable" and to expect an "amazing two days" ahead. Follow live.

image.altText: A US military ship

items.title: Australian dollar surges to four-year high as traders see end to Iran war

children.content: Analysts say the mood on global financial markets has improved considerably over the past few days.

image.altText: Gold dollar coins on bank notes.

items.title: Hanson wishes Coalition good luck, government focuses on fuel

children.content: This week in Canberra, Pauline Hanson learned imitation is the sincerest form of flattery, the defence budget was on the agenda and a fire threw the fuel crisis into sharp relief.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson

items.title: Is LIV Golf dead? Not yet, but the vultures are circling

children.content: Rumours of LIV Golf's immediate demise look wide of the mark, but the smoke is only growing thicker as denials from the league's CEO help paint a bleak picture.

image.altText: Cameron Smith at a LIV Golf press conference

items.title: Bombs, rain and destruction: Life in Gaza six months into a ceasefire

children.content: Palestinians in Gaza fear they are being forgotten as the Iran war drags on, with Israeli strikes continuing despite a ceasefire and the humanitarian situation still dire.

gallery.altText: A woman wearing a hijab sits on a chair in front of tents  on dirt.
dozens of make shift tents on muddy dirt ground

items.title: What we know about the cause and impact of the Viva Energy oil refinery fire

children.content: Firefighters have managed to extinguish a blaze at one of Australia's major oil refineries after it burnt for 12 hours. Here's what we know so far about the cause of the fire — and the possible impact on fuel prices.

image.altText: fire and smoke pictured above the Geelong refinery in the evening

items.title: ‘How long is a piece of string?’: The challenge of reopening a refinery

children.content: A former refinery safety manager says preventing fires is hardwired into refining plants.

image.altText: Smoke rises off the refinery building at Corio

items.title: Prince Harry to headline mental health summit on day three of Australian visit

children.content: The Duke and Duchess of Sussex are back together in Melbourne after spending the second day of their Australian tour apart.

items.title: Explosions heard during fire in Byron Bay bike shop

children.content: The northern NSW town was bustling with holiday-makers enjoying the Easter school holidays when the fire broke out in the CBD this afternoon.

image.altText: A group of fire fighters hose down after battling a blaze

items.title: Underworld figure had 'never' sat outside at cafe until day he was shot, court told

children.content: Victoria's Supreme Court is told underworld figure Gavin Preston was cautious about his surroundings and "never" sat outside a cafe he regularly attended — other than the day he was shot dead.

image.altText: Crime figure Gavin Preston looks unsmilingly into the camera as he poses for a photo.

items.title: New data reveals age group spending up big

children.content: Australians spent more in March as the outbreak of the Iran war drove up petrol prices. Older Australians increased their spending at the fastest pace, CBA data shows.

image.altText: A close-up of $100 notes fanning out from a wallet.

items.title: Liberty Bell Bay workers told to take leave without pay or be sacked

children.content: About 175 workers at Australia's only manganese smelter, Liberty Bell Bay, have been told they will be laid off at the end of next week unless they take leave without pay.

image.altText: A blue sign with a flame on it and the word 'LIBERTY' underneath, on the side of the road.

items.title: How to tell if your dog is in pain and what to do if they are

children.content: New research suggests that many dog owners are not skilled in recognising pain in their pets.

gallery.altText: A brown dog with floppy ears.

thumbnail.altText: A brown dog with floppy ears.

items.title: Rebel Wilson's The Deb is a delight despite the ongoing legal drama

children.content: Plus a powerful look Palestinian life through multiple generations and a buzzy Japanese video game adaptation makes it to Australian shores.

image.altText: Three women stand in front of a stage with blue and red velvet curtains

items.title: Quiz: Can you tell the difference between The Pitt and ER?

children.content: Ahead of The Pitt season finale dropping this week, can you pick the difference between it and its hospital drama ancestor ER?

image.altText: Composite image of Noah Wyle as a young and older doctor

items.title: Video shows father and son illegally scuttling boat off NSW South Coast

children.content: In a historic judgment, the two fishermen are fined $15,000 for towing their vessel out to sea and illegally scuttling it.

gallery.altText: Trawler sinking at sea as fishermen watch on from another vessel.

thumbnail.altText: Trawler sinking at sea as fishermen watch on from another vessel.

items.title: NSW premier told to resign after protest laws struck down by top court

children.content: The laws, rushed through parliament after the Bondi Beach terrorist attack, have been found to be unconstitutional by the state's highest court.

image.altText: A large group of police and riot squad officers push back a crowd of protesters in the Sydney CBD.

items.title: Russian missiles hit Kyiv and other Ukrainian cities, 12 dead

children.content: Russian forces have attacked the Ukrainian capital Kyiv and other cities, killing 12 people, as the defence leaders of more than 50 countries say they are aiming to send more aid to Ukraine.

image.altText: Two firefighters work as huge orange flames and black smoke fill the night sky, at a recycling centre.

items.title: Man, 53, charged over seizure of more than 350 cannabis plants

children.content: A 53-year-old Kalgoorlie man is released on bail after the seizure of more than 350 cannabis plants from a property WA Police allege had been converted for the purpose of growing hydroponic cannabis.

image.altText: Photos of a hydronic cannabis operation supplied by police.

items.title: Emergency services battling fire in Brisbane's east

children.content: Emergency services were called to Hemmant just after 3pm, with more than a dozen crews attending the scene.

image.altText: A large amount of smoke comes from a building with a fire truck parked on the side of the road.

items.title: Arrest warrant issued in legal battle for $27k in unpaid long service leave

children.content: It's now been over three years since two women left their jobs at a small business. Two courts later, they want their combined $27,000 in long service leave paid out to them, and say they won't give up until they get it.

gallery.altText: two women sitting at a dining table looking through paperwork

thumbnail.altText: two women sitting at a dining table looking through paperwork

items.title: Unemployment rate remains at 4.3pc in 'calm before the storm'

children.content: Australia's unemployment rate remained at 4.3 per cent in March, but economists say cracks could start appearing in coming months.

image.altText: Pedestrians cross at Flinders Street

items.title: Pandemic, 'ice epidemic' worsened Qld child safety pressures, ex-minister says

children.content: An inquiry into Queensland's child safety system hears the former minister was made aware of the sector's resource issues, as well as the "significant increase" in children entering residential care.

image.altText: A close-up of a woman with dark hair

items.title: Former Launceston mayor resigns from council after scandal-plagued term

children.content: The former mayor and councillor of Tasmanian's second-largest city resigns effective immediately, years after an ABC investigation uncovered historical allegations about his conduct towards young men in the theatre scene.

image.altText: A portrait shot of a smiling man in gold and black mayoral robes.

items.title: AFL 'firestorm' taking heavy toll on players, Saints coach says

children.content: St Kilda coach Ross Lyon says the damage from a "firestorm" around the AFL Tribunal is weighing heavily as his club signals it may appeal against Lance Collard's suspension.

image.altText: A St Kilda AFL coach stands on the ground looking downfield ahead of a match.

items.title: Australia's rarest reptile found after being considered extinct

children.content: Not found anywhere else on Earth, a skink considered functionally extinct has been found in a national park in far west NSW.
